contributions.detail: Buyer is actively deep-diving an unidentified alternative 'eternal I/O provider,' which directly triggered the POC pause (11-May-2026) — a live, undefined competitive threat, not a named-and-contained rival.
No direct or indirect engagement from EB Eduardo Viera in ~9 months; CFO Joshua Jepsen has never engaged — decision path and budget sign-off remain unmapped.

Forecast sits at Best Case despite the POC being explicitly paused and close date pushed 145 days — the field looks over-positioned versus hard evidence. ► Correct forecast category until Karen/Eduardo confirm a path back.
Karen personally validated positive POC results for ANA and Spend Analysis (Mar-2026) — a genuine, buyer-voiced proof point to rebuild the case on.

Buyer challenged Zycus's core ROI claim (40% cycle-time reduction) as 'pretty optimistic' on 18-Dec-2025 and asked for benchmarks — business case still unproven. ► Bring validated benchmark data to the next connect.
Multi-quarter, multi-workshop history (12 meetings, 4 with transcripts) shows real depth was reached at Shortlisted, giving a re-engagement floor if the July connect lands.
Karen Pagorek told Zycus on 11-May-2026 the POC 'will not move forward' while Deere re-assesses priorities — a buyer-initiated stop, not a rep slowdown.

Zero buyer meetings in 30 days and only one in 60 days (11-May); no confirmed future meeting is on the calendar despite next-step notes referencing an 'accepted' 20-Jul connect. ► Verify whether that call actually happened and log the outcome.
Close date has been pushed 145 days (Sep-2026 → Feb-2027), a hard drag signal on top of the stall. ► Re-baseline close date only once real re-engagement is confirmed.
Economic buyer Eduardo Viera has been silent ~9 months with no cadence-driving contact above Karen. ► Force the Eduardo connection the rep has flagged repeatedly.

Deep engagement history exists (12 meetings incl. workshops and a delivered POC on 31-Mar) — a real base to reactivate rather than cold outreach. ► Lead the 20-Jul touchpoint with the earlier ANA/Spend POC wins.
Buyer unilaterally halted the POC ('will not move forward') to re-assess internal priorities — commitment is retreating, not advancing. ► Get Karen to define what re-starts it.
Paper process is a confirmed MEDDPICC gap — no procurement/security review or reference requests in evidence. ► Push for a paper-process kickoff once re-engaged.
Karen and her team personally ran and validated the ANA/Spend Analysis POC — real internal effort was invested, not just interest.
Investment is concentrated in one contact (Karen); Eduardo and the CFO have never reviewed or engaged with the business case. ► Broaden commitment beyond a single champion.
Buyer touches are thin — 1 touch in both the 30d and 60d windows, no recent buyer-initiated cadence.

Active, unidentified competitive threat: Deere is deep-diving an alternative I/O provider that directly caused the Zycus POC to pause (11-May-2026). ► Identify the alternative and counter-position fast.
Close date pushed 145 days (Sep-2026 → Feb-2027) while forecast still shows Best Case — conviction is not supported by current evidence. ► Correct the forecast category to reflect reality.
Economic buyer Eduardo Viera silent ~9 months, CFO never engaged — no confirmed budget or commercial sign-off pathway.

Rep's own next-step notes flag the real possible outcomes as 'on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss' — this is a self-identified risk, not analyst speculation. ► Get a definitive answer at the 20-Jul connect.
Relationship depth (12 meetings, POC delivered, positive ANA/Spend results) means the deal is recoverable if Karen/Eduardo re-engage rather than a cold restart.
